sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 4.66 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.4)

statement.latex: Let$P$be a presentation of a finite group$G$on$m_p$generators and$r_p$relations. The deficiency\/${ def}\, (G)$is the maximum of$m_p - r_p$over all presentations$P$. Let$G$be a finite group such that$G=G' 1$and the multiplicator$M(G)=1$. Prove that${ def}\, (G^n) -$as$n$, where$G^n$is the$n$th direct power of~$G$.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
